agent_scopes.mechanism: project (.kiro/agents/) + global (~/.kiro/agents/); global overrides local on name collision — reverse of most Kiro content types

definition_format.mechanism: JSON config files in .kiro/agents/ (project) or ~/.kiro/agents/ (global); prompt field accepts inline string or file:// URI to external .md file

invocation_patterns.mechanism: keyboard shortcut binding for IDE invocation; welcomeMessage shown on agent start; manual selection via agent picker

model_selection.mechanism: model field in agent JSON config overrides workspace default for that agent

per_agent_mcp.mechanism: inline mcpServers map per agent config; includeMcpJson boolean controls whether workspace .kiro/mcp.json is merged into agent's server set

subagent_spawning.mechanism: no subagent spawning mechanism documented for Kiro custom agents

tool_restrictions.mechanism: tools array with exact names, wildcards (computer_*), or MCP-namespaced refs (mcp:serverName:toolName); toolsSettings for per-tool config objects

providerExtensions.name: File URI for externalizing agent prompt
providerExtensions.description: The 'prompt' field accepts either an inline string or a 'file://path/to/system-prompt.md' URI to load the prompt from an external Markdown file. Enables long or complex system prompts to be maintained as separate files without embedding them in the JSON config.

providerExtensions.name: toolAliases for collision remapping
providerExtensions.description: A 'toolAliases' map that renames tools to resolve naming collisions. When two MCP servers expose tools with the same name, toolAliases provides a per-agent remapping so both tools remain accessible under distinct names.

providerExtensions.name: Resources attachment (files, skills, knowledge-base)
providerExtensions.description: Agents can declare a 'resources' array attaching contextual inputs: individual files (type: file, path), skills/powers (type: skill, name), or knowledge-base entries (type: knowledge-base, name). Resources are loaded into the agent's context alongside its system prompt.

providerExtensions.name: Inline mcpServers map per agent
providerExtensions.description: Agents can declare their own 'mcpServers' map directly in the agent JSON config (same schema as .kiro/mcp.json), scoping specific MCP server configurations to that agent without affecting the workspace-wide MCP config.

providerExtensions.name: includeMcpJson flag
providerExtensions.description: A boolean flag ('includeMcpJson') that controls whether the workspace .kiro/mcp.json config is merged into this agent's available MCP servers. Defaults to true; set to false to give an agent an isolated server set using only its inline mcpServers.

providerExtensions.name: toolsSettings per-tool configuration map
providerExtensions.description: A 'toolsSettings' map that provides per-tool configuration objects, allowing fine-grained behavioral settings for individual tools available to the agent. Tool-specific options vary by tool type.

providerExtensions.name: keyboardShortcut binding
providerExtensions.description: Agents can declare a 'keyboardShortcut' string to bind a keyboard shortcut for quick agent invocation from the IDE. IDE-level keybinding, not relevant to portable agent config.

providerExtensions.name: welcomeMessage shown on agent start
providerExtensions.description: An optional 'welcomeMessage' string displayed to the user when the agent session starts. Provides onboarding context or usage instructions at session open.
